site.name: ErrorLookup
site.defaultTitle: HTTP Status Code Lookup, Meanings, Causes, and Fixes
site.defaultDescription: Lookup HTTP status codes, understand what they mean, compare related errors, and find fixes for common server, client, and redirect issues.

codes.title: HTTP 100 Continue
codes.summary: HTTP 100 Continue indicates a informational response outcome.
codes.meaning: Continue describes how the server processed the request and what the client should do next.

codes.title: HTTP 300 Multiple Choices
codes.summary: HTTP 300 Multiple Choices indicates a redirection response outcome.
codes.meaning: Multiple Choices describes how the server processed the request and what the client should do next.
causes: Resource moved to a different URI
fixes: Use the correct permanent vs temporary code

codes.title: HTTP 301 Moved Permanently
codes.summary: HTTP 301 Moved Permanently indicates a redirection response outcome.
codes.meaning: Moved Permanently describes how the server processed the request and what the client should do next.
fixes: Point links to the destination URL

codes.title: HTTP 302 Found
codes.summary: HTTP 302 Found indicates a redirection response outcome.
codes.meaning: Found describes how the server processed the request and what the client should do next.
fixes: Test method preservation behavior where relevant

codes.title: HTTP 306 Unused

codes.title: HTTP 404 Not Found
codes.summary: HTTP 404 Not Found indicates a client errors response outcome.
codes.meaning: Not Found describes how the server processed the request and what the client should do next.
fixes: Restore the resource if it was removed unintentionally
Set up a redirect if the page moved

faq.q: Does 404 mean the server is down?
faq.a: No. It means the resource was not found at that URL.
Only when there is a relevant replacement resource.
